err != nil {\n224\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_PRESIGN_SECRET must be hex-encoded: %w\", err)\n225\t\t\t}\n226\t\t\tif len(cfg.PresignSecret) < 32 {\n227\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_PRESIGN_SECRET must be at least 32 bytes (64 hex chars)\")\n228\t\t\t}\n229\t\t} else {\n230\t\t\t// Use the auth secret key as the presign secret if not specified\n231\t\t\tcfg.PresignSecret = []byte(cfg.AuthSecretKey)\n232\t\t}\n233\t\tcfg.PresignBaseURL = os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_PRESIGN_BASE_URL\")\n234\t\tif cfg.PresignBaseURL == \"\" {\n235\t\t\t// Default to /share path on the main listener\n236\t\t\tcfg.PresignBaseURL = \"/share\"\n237\t\t}\n238\t\n239\t\t// Load named keys (ARMOR_MEK_<NAME>)\n240\t\tcfg.NamedKeys = make(map[string][]byte)\n241\t\tfor _, env := range os.Environ() {\n242\t\t\t// Look for ARMOR_MEK_<NAME> pattern\n243\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(env, \"ARMOR_MEK_\") {\n244\t\t\t\tparts := strings.SplitN(env, \"=\", 2)\n245\t\t\t\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n246\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n247\t\t\t\t}\n248\t\t\t\tname := strings.TrimPrefix(parts[0], \"ARMOR_MEK_\")\n249\t\t\t\tname = strings.ToLower(name)\n250\t\t\t\tif name == \"\" {\n251\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n252\t\t\t\t}\n253\t\t\t\t// Decode hex MEK\n254\t\t\t\tmek, err := hex.DecodeString(parts[1])\n255\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n256\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_MEK_%s must be hex-encoded: %w\", name, err)\n257\t\t\t\t}\n258\t\t\t\tif len(mek) != 32 {\n259\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_MEK_%s must be 32 bytes (64 hex chars), got %d bytes\", name, len(mek))\n260\t\t\t\t}\n261\t\t\t\tcfg.NamedKeys[name] = mek\n262\t\t\t}\n263\t\t}\n264\t\n265\t\t// Load key routes (ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES)\n266\t\tif routesStr := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES\"); routesStr != \"\" {\n267\t\t\troutes, err := parseKeyRoutes(routesStr)\n268\t\t\tif err != nil {\n269\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES: nil\n280\t}\n281\t\n282\tfunc getEnv(key, defaultVal string) string {\n283\t\tif val := os.Getenv(key); val != \"\" {\n284\t\t\treturn val\n285\t\t}\n286\t\treturn defaultVal\n287\t}\n288\t\n289\tfunc getEnvInt(key string, defaultVal int) int {\n290\t\tif val := os.Getenv(key); val != \"\" {\n291\t\t\tif i, err := strconv.Atoi(val); err == nil {\n292\t\t\t\treturn i\n293\t\t\t}\n294\t\t}\n295\t\treturn defaultVal\n296\t}\n297\t\n298\t// normalizePrefix normalizes a prefix string according to ADR-001.\n299\t// - Removes leading slashes\n300\t// - Ensures exactly one trailing slash\n301\t// - Empty string stays empty (no prefix)\n302\t// Examples:\n303\t//   \"\" → \"\"\n304\t//   \"kalshi-tape\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n305\t//   \"kalshi-tape/\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n306\t//   \"/kalshi-tape\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n307\t//   \"/kalshi-tape/\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n308\t//   \"kalshi-tape//\" → \"kalshi-tape/\"\n309\tfunc normalizePrefix(prefix string) string {\n310\t\tif prefix == \"\" {\n311\t\t\treturn \"\"\n312\t\t}\n313\t\n314\t\t// Remove leading slashes\n315\t\tprefix = strings.TrimLeft(prefix, \"/\")\n316\t\n317\t\t// Remove all trailing slashes first\n318\t\tprefix = strings.TrimRight(prefix, \"/\")\n319\t\n320\t\t// Add exactly one trailing slash if non-empty\n321\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n322\t\t\tprefix += \"/\"\n323\t\t}\n324\t\n325\t\treturn prefix\n326\t}\n327\t\n328\tfunc generateRandomKey(length int) string {\n329\t\tb := make([]byte, length)\n330\t\tif _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {\n331\t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to generate random key: %v\", err))\n332\t\t}\n333\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(b)\n334\t}\n335\t\n336\t// parseKeyRoutes parses a key routes string.\n337\t// Format: \"prefix1=key1,prefix2=key2,*=default\"\n338\t// The * prefix is a catch-all that maps to the default key.\n339\tfunc parseKeyRoutes(routesStr string) ([]KeyRoute, error) {\n340\t\tif routesStr == \"\" {\n341\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n342\t\t}\n343\t\n344\t\tvar routes []KeyRoute\n345\t\tparts := strings.Split(routesStr, \",\")\n346\t\n347\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n348\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n349\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n350\t\t\t\tcontinue\n351\t\t\t}\n352\t\n353\t\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(part, \"=\", 2)\n354\t\t\tif len(kv) != 2 {\n355\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid route format %q (expected prefix=keyname)\", part)\n356\t\t\t}\n357\t\n358\t\t\tprefix := strings.TrimSpace(kv[0])\n359\t\t\tkeyName := strings.TrimSpace(kv[1])\n360\t\n361\t\t\tif prefix == \"\" || keyName == \"\" {\n362\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid route %q (empty prefix or key name)\", part)\n363\t\t\t}\n364\t\n365\t\t\t// Handle wildcard - it maps to default key\n366\t\t\tif prefix == \"*\" {\n367\t\t\t\tprefix = \"\"\n368\t\t\t}\n369\t\n370\t\t\troutes = append(routes, KeyRoute{\n371\t\t\t\tPrefix:  prefix,\n372\t\t\t\tKeyName: keyName,\n373\t\t\t})\n374\t\t}\n375\t\n376\t\treturn routes, nil\n377\t}\n378\t\n379\t// loadNamedCredentials loads additional named credentials from environment variables.\n380\t// Format: ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACCESS_KEY, ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_SECRET_KEY, ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACL\n381\t// Named credentials must have a non-empty NAME that doesn't conflict with default credential names.\n382\tfunc loadNamedCredentials(cfg *Config) error {\n383\t\t// Collect all credential names\n384\t\tcredNames := make(map[string]bool)\n385\t\tfor _, env := range os.Environ() {\n386\t\t\t// Look for ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACCESS_KEY pattern where NAME is not empty\n387\t\t\t// and not one of the default credential env vars\n388\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(env, \"ARMOR_AUTH_\") && strings.Contains(env, \"_ACCESS_KEY=\") {\n389\t\t\t\tparts := strings.SplitN(env, \"=\", 2)\n390\t\t\t\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n391\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n392\t\t\t\t}\n393\t\t\t\t// Extract name: ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACCESS_KEY -> <NAME>\n394\t\t\t\tenvKey := parts[0]\n395\t\t\t\t// Skip the default credential env var\n396\t\t\t\tif envKey == \"ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY\" {\n397\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n398\t\t\t\t}\n399\t\t\t\tnamePart := strings.TrimPrefix(envKey, \"ARMOR_AUTH_\")\n400\t\t\t\tnamePart = strings.TrimSuffix(namePart, \"_ACCESS_KEY\")\n401\t\t\t\tif namePart == \"\" {\n402\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n403\t\t\t\t}\n404\t\t\t\tcredNames[namePart] = true\n405\t\t\t}\n406\t\t}\n407\t\n408\t\t// Load each credential\n409\t\tfor name := range credNames {\n410\t\t\taccessKey := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_AUTH_\" + name + \"_ACCESS_KEY\")\n411\t\t\tsecretKey := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_AUTH_\" + name + \"_SECRET_KEY\")\n412\t\t\taclStr := os.Getenv(\"ARMOR_AUTH_\" + name + \"_ACL\")\n413\t\n414\t\t\tif accessKey == \"\" || secretKey == \"\" {\n415\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"ARMOR_AUTH_%s_ACCESS_KEY and ARMOR_AUTH_%s_SECRET_KEY are both required\", name, name)\n416\t\t\t}\n417\t\n418\t\t\tcred := &Credential{\n419\t\t\t\tAccessKey: accessKey,\n420\t\t\t\tSecretKey: cfg.Credentials[accessKey]; exists {\n434\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"duplicate access key in ARMOR_AUTH_%s\", name)\n435\t\t\t}\n436\t\n437\t\t\tcfg.Credentials[accessKey] = cred\n438\t\t}\n439\t\n440\t\treturn nil\n441\t}\n442\t\n443\t// parseACL parses an ACL string into ACL entries.\n444\t// Format: \"bucket1:prefix1,bucket2:prefix2,bucket3:*\"\n445\t// A bucket of \"*\" means all buckets.\n446\t// A prefix of \"*\" or \"\" means any prefix within the bucket.\n447\tfunc parseACL(aclStr string) ([]ACLEntry, error) {\n448\t\tif aclStr == \"\" {\n449\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n450\t\t}\n451\t\n452\t\tvar entries []ACLEntry\n453\t\tparts := strings.Split(aclStr, \",\")\n454\t\n455\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n456\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n457\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n458\t\t\t\tcontinue\n459\t\t\t}\n460\t\n461\t\t\t// Split bucket:prefix\n462\t\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(part, \":\", 2)\n463\t\t\tif len(kv) != 2 {\n464\t\t\t\treturn nil, len(entries) == 0 {\n486\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"ACL string contains no valid entries\")\n487\t\t}\n488\t\n489\t\treturn entries, nil\n490\t}\n491\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_55c2a797caf54544b808f918","session_id":"87d1ceba-3753-46c2-8019-12a1aa96818c","uuid":"be9c484e-e37e-4621-af44-6cef8e74161d","timestamp":"2026-07-15T16:20:06.340Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=...\n62\tfunc ParseAuthHeader(auth string) (*AuthHeader, error) {\n63\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(auth, \"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 \") {\n64\t\t\treturn nil, *http.Request, body []byte) (*config.Credential, error) {\n117\t\tauthHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Authorization\")\n118\t\tif authHeader == \"\" {\n119\t\t\treturn nil, canonicalRequest)\n154\t\n155\t\t// Calculate signature using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n156\t\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, parsed.CredentialDate, parsed.Region)\n157\t\tcalculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n158\t\n159\t\t// Compare signatures (constant-time comparison would be better but hex strings are not sensitive)\n160\t\tif calculatedSig != parsed.Signature {\n161\t\t\treturn nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n162\t\t}\n163\t\n164\t\treturn cred, nil\n165\t}\n166\t\n167\t// buildCanonicalRequest builds the canonical request string per AWS spec.\n168\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalRequest(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string, body []byte) string {\n169\t\t// 1. \"\\n\")\n205\t}\n206\t\n207\t// buildCanonicalQueryString builds the canonical query string.\n208\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalQueryString(query url.Values) string {\n209\t\tif len(query) == 0 {\n210\t\t\treturn \"\"\n211\t\t}\n212\t\n213\t\t// Sort keys\n214\t\tkeys := make([]string, 0, len(query))\n215\t\tfor k := range query {\n216\t\t\tkeys = append(keys, r.Host\n241\t\t\t\t// Include port if it's non-standard\n242\t\t\t\tif r.URL.Port() != \"\" && r.URL.Port() != \"80\" && r.URL.Port() != \"443\" {\n243\t\t\t\t\thost = r.Host\n244\t\t\t\t}\n245\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"host:\"+strings.TrimSpace(host))\n246\t\t\t\tcontinue\n247\t\t\t}\n248\t\n249\t\t\t// Get header values and join with commas\n250\t\t\tvalues := r.Header.Values(h)\n251\t\t\tif len(values) == 0 {\n252\t\t\t\t// Check for X-Amz-Date as x-amz-date\n253\t\t\t\tif strings.ToLower(h) == \"x-amz-date\" {\n254\t\t\t\t\tif v := r.Header.Get(\"X-Amz-Date\"); v != \"\" {\n255\t\t\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, \"x-amz-date:\"+strings.TrimSpace(v))\n256\t\t\t\t\t}\n257\t\t\t\t}\n258\t\t\t\tcontinue\n259\t\t\t}\n260\t\n261\t\t\t// Trim leading/trailing whitespace and collapse multiple spaces\n262\t\t\ttrimmedVals := make([]string, len(values))\n263\t\t\tfor i, v := range values {\n264\t\t\t\ttrimmedVals[i] = strings.Join(strings.Fields(v), \" \")\n265\t\t\t}\n266\t\t\tlines = append(lines, h+\":\"+strings.Join(trimmedVals, \",\"))\n267\t\t}\n268\t\n269\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\") + \"\\n\"\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\t// buildStringToSign builds the string to sign per AWS spec.\n273\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildStringToSign(amzDate, credentialDate, region, canonicalRequest string) string {\n274\t\tcredentialScope := credentialDate + \"/\" + region + \"/\" + a.service + \"/aws4_request\"\n275\t\n276\t\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n277\t\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256\",\n278\t\t\tamzDate,\n279\t\t\tcredentialScope,\n280\t\t\tsha256Hex([]byte(canonicalRequest)),\n281\t\t}, \"\\n\")\n282\t}\n283\t\n284\t// getSigningKeyForCredential derives the signing key for the given credential, date, and region.\n285\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) getSigningKeyForCredential(cred *config.Credential, date, region string) []byte {\n286\t\tkDate := a.hmacSHA256([]byte(\"AWS4\"+cred.SecretKey), date)\n287\t\tkRegion := a.hmacSHA256(kDate, region)\n288\t\tkService := a.hmacSHA256(kRegion, a.service)\n289\t\tkSigning := a.hmacSHA256(kService, \"aws4_request\")\n290\t\treturn kSigning\n291\t}\n292\t\n293\t// CheckACL verifies that the credential is allowed to access the given bucket and key.\n294\t// If the credential has no ACLs (nil), it has full access.\n295\tfunc CheckACL(cred *config.Credential, bucket, key string) error {\n296\t\t// No ACLs means full access\n297\t\tif len(cred.ACLs) == 0 {\n298\t\t\treturn nil\n299\t\t}\n300\t\n301\t\tfor _, acl := range cred.ACLs {\n302\t\t\t// Check bucket match\n303\t\t\tif acl.Bucket != \"*\" && acl.Bucket != bucket HMAC-SHA256.\n321\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) hmacSHA256(key []byte, data string) []byte {\n322\t\th := hmac.New(sha256.New, key)\n323\t\th.Write([]byte(data))\n324\t\treturn h.Sum(nil)\n325\t}\n326\t\n327\t// sha256Sum computes SHA256 hash as hex string.\n328\tfunc sha256Sum(data []byte) string {\n329\t\treturn sha256Hex(data)\n330\t}\n331\t\n332\t// sha256Hex computes SHA256 hash and returns hex encoding.\n333\tfunc sha256Hex(data []byte) string {\n334\t\thash := sha256.Sum256(data)\n335\t\treturn hex.EncodeToString(hash[:])\n336\t}\n337\t\n338\t// Authentication VerifyQueryAuth verifies SigV4 authentication via query parameters (presigned URLs).\n363\t// Returns the credential if verification succeeds.\n364\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) VerifyQueryAuth(r *http.Request) (*config.Credential, error) {\n365\t\t// Extract query parameters\n366\t\tquery := r.URL.Query()\n367\t\n368\t\taccessKeyCred := query.Get(\"X-Amz-Credential\")\n369\t\tif accessKeyCred == \"\" {\n370\t\t\treturn nil, ErrMissingAuthHeader\n371\t\t}\n372\t\n373\t\t// Parse credential\n374\t\tcredParts := strings.Split(accessKeyCred, \"/\")\n375\t\tif len(credParts) != 5 strconv.Atoi(expires)\n408\t\t\tif err != nil {\n409\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n410\t\t\t}\n411\t\n412\t\t\trequestTime, err := time.Parse(\"20060102T150405Z\", amzDate)\n413\t\t\tif err != nil {\n414\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrInvalidDateFormat\n415\t\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\t\tif time.Since(requestTime) > time.Duration(expiresSec)*time.Second {\n418\t\t\t\treturn nil, ErrRequestExpired\n419\t\t\t}\n420\t\t}\n421\t\n422\t\t// For query auth, the body is typically empty for GET requests\n423\t\tbody := []byte{}\n424\t\n425\t\t// Build canonical request (excluding signature from query)\n426\t\tcanonicalRequest := a.buildCanonicalQueryRequest(r, signedHeaders, body, query)\n427\t\n428\t\t// Build string to sign using the region the client claimed\n429\t\tcredentialDate := credParts[1]\n430\t\tclientRegion := credParts[2]\n431\t\tstringToSign := a.buildStringToSign(amzDate, credentialDate, clientRegion, canonicalRequest)\n432\t\n433\t\t// Calculate signature using the credential's secret key and the client's claimed region\n434\t\tsigningKey := a.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, credentialDate, clientRegion)\n435\t\tcalculatedSig := hex.EncodeToString(a.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n436\t\n437\t\tif calculatedSig != signature {\n438\t\t\treturn nil, ErrSignatureMismatch\n439\t\t}\n440\t\n441\t\treturn cred, nil\n442\t}\n443\t\n444\t// buildCanonicalQueryRequest builds canonical request for query-based auth.\n445\tfunc (a *SigV4Auth) buildCanonicalQueryRequest(r *http.Request, signedHeaders []string, body []byte, query url.Values) string {\n446\t\t// Create a copy of query params without auth-related ones\n447\t\tcanonicalQuery := make(url.Values)\n448\t\tfor k, v := range query {\n449\t\t\t// Skip auth-related params\n450\t\t\tif k == \"X-Amz-Signature\" {\n451\t\t\t\tcontinue\n452\t\t\t}\n453\t\t\tcanonicalQuery[k] = v\n454\t\t}\n455\t\n456\t\t// Build canonical query string\n457\t\tqueryStr := a.buildCanonicalQueryString(canonicalQuery)\n458\t\n459\t\t// Build canonical request\n460\t\tpath := r.URL.EscapedPath()\n461\t\tif path == \"\" {\n462\t\t\tpath = \"/\"\n463\t\t}\n464\t\n465\t\tcanonicalHeaders := a.buildCanonicalHeaders(r, signedHeaders)\n466\t\tsignedHeadersStr := strings.Join(signedHeaders, \";\")\n467\t\tpayloadHash := sha256Sum(body)\n468\t\n469\t\treturn strings.Join([]string{\n470\t\t\tr.Method,\n471\t\t\tpath,\n472\t\t\tqueryStr,\n473\t\t\tcanonicalHeaders,\n474\t\t\tsignedHeadersStr,\n475\t\t\tpayloadHash,\n476\t\t}, \"\\n\")\n477\t}\n478\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_55c2a797caf54544b808f918","session_id":"87d1ceba-3753-46c2-8019-12a1aa96818c","uuid":"d69b3b49-3bce-4a36-a67a-7fa6a0af4fc9","timestamp":"2026-07-15T16:20:06.470Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore auth.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, \"20130524\", \"us-east-1\")\n201\t\tresult := fmt.Sprintf(\"%x\", signingKey)\n202\t\n203\t\tif result != expectedKey {\n204\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected signing key %s, got %s\", expectedKey, result)\n205\t\t}\n206\t}\n207\t\n208\tfunc TestHmacSHA256(t *testing.T) {\n209\t\tauth := &SigV4Auth{}\n210\t\n211\t\t// Test vector: HMAC-SHA256 of \"AWS4wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY\" with \"20130524\"\n212\t\t// From AWS documentation\n213\t\tkey := []byte(\"AWS4wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY\")\n214\t\tresult := auth.hmacSHA256(key, \"20130524\")\n215\t\n216\t\t// This should produce a specific hash\n217\t\tif len(result) != 32 {\n218\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"expected 32 bytes, got %d\", len(result))\n219\t\t}\n220\t}\n221\t\n222\tfunc TestVerifyRequest_InvalidAuth(t *testing.T) {\n223\t\tauth := NewSigV4Auth(\"test-access-key\", \"test-secret-key\", \"us-east-1\")\n224\t\n225\t\ttests := []struct {\n226\t\t\tname        string\n227\t\t\tsetupReq    func() *http.Request\n228\t\t\texpectError error\n229\t\t}{\n230\t\t\t{\n231\t\t\t\tname: \"missing err)\n289\t\t\t\t}\n290\t\t\t})\n291\t\t}\n292\t}\n293\t\n294\t// TestVerifyRequest_ValidSignature tests with a properly signed request.\n295\t// This uses the signature calculation logic to verify end-to-end signing works.\n296\tfunc TestVerifyRequest_ValidSignature(t *testing.T) {\n297\t\taccessKey := \"AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE\"\n298\t\tsecretKey := \"wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY\"\n299\t\tregion := \"us-east-1\"\n300\t\n301\t\tauth := NewSigV4Auth(accessKey, secretKey, region)\n302\t\n303\t\t// Create a request with current timestamp\n304\t\tnow := time.Now().UTC()\n305\t\tamzDate := now.Format(\"20060102T150405Z\")\n306\t\tcredentialDate := now.Format(\"20060102\")\n307\t\n308\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"GET\", \"https://examplebucket.s3.amazonaws.com/test.txt\", nil)\n309\t\treq.Host = \"examplebucket.s3.amazonaws.com\"\n310\t\treq.Header.Set(\"X-Amz-Date\", amzDate)\n311\t\n312\t\tbody := []byte{}\n313\t\n314\t\tcred := &config.Credential{\n315\t\t\tAccessKey: accessKey,\n316\t\t\tSecretKey: secretKey,\n317\t\t}\n318\t\n319\t\t// Build canonical request\n320\t\tcanonicalRequest := auth.buildCanonicalRequest(req, []string{\"host\", \"x-amz-date\"}, body)\n321\t\n322\t\t// Build string to sign\n323\t\tstringToSign := auth.buildStringToSign(amzDate, credentialDate, region, canonicalRequest)\n324\t\n325\t\t// Calculate signature\n326\t\tsigningKey := auth.getSigningKeyForCredential(cred, credentialDate, region)\n327\t\tcalculatedSig := fmt.Sprintf(\"%x\", auth.hmacSHA256(signingKey, stringToSign))\n328\t\n329\t\t// Set the authorization header with the calculated signature\n330\t\tauthHeader := fmt.Sprintf(\n331\t\t\t\"AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 Credential=%s/%s/%s/s3/aws4_request, SignedHeaders=host;x-amz-date, Signature=%s\",\n332\t\t\taccessKey, credentialDate, region, calculatedSig,\n333\t\t)\n334\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Authorization\", authHeader)\n335\t\n336\t\t// Verify\n337\t\t_, err := auth.VerifyRequest(req, body)\n338\t\tif err != nil {\n339\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"signature verification failed: %v\", err)\n340\t\t}\n341\t}\n342\t\n343\tfunc TestVerifyRequest_WithBody(t *testing.T) {\n344\t\taccessKey := \"test-access-key\"\n345\t\tsecretKey := \"test-secret-key\"\n346\t\tregion := TestVerifyRequest_AnyRegionAcceptable(t *testing.T) {\n516\t\taccessKey := \"AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE\"\n517\t\tsecretKey := \"wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY\"\n518\t\tserverRegion := \"us-west-002\"\n519\t\n520\t\t// Server is configured with us-west-002, but clients may sign with any region.\n521\t\tclientRegions := []string{\"us-west-002\", \"auto\", \"us-east-1\", {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_dfae5fb9aca94062a0eaea6b","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t# ARMOR\n2\t\n3\t**Authenticated Range-readable Managed Object Repository**\n4\t\n5\tARMOR is an S3-compatible proxy server that transparently encrypts data before storing it in [Backblaze B2](https://www.backblaze.com/cloud-storage) and serves downloads through Cloudflare for zero-egress cost. Any S3-compatible client — boto3, AWS CLI, DuckDB, rclone — works without modification.\n6\t\n7\t- **Zero-knowledge encryption** — data is encrypted before it leaves ARMOR; B2 only ever stores ciphertext\n8\t- **Zero egress fees** — downloads route through Cloudflare via the Bandwidth Alliance\n9\t- **Seekable encryption** — AES-256-CTR with 64KB blocks enables byte-range reads without decrypting the whole file\n10\t- **DuckDB-compatible** — query encrypted Parquet files with column pruning and predicate pushdown intact\n11\t- **Multi-key routing** — different master keys for different path prefixes; automatic key selection per object\n12\t\n13\t## Quick Start\n14\t\n15\t### Docker\n16\t\n17\t```bash\n18\tdocker run -d \\\n19\t  -p 9000:9000 \\\n20\t  -p 9001:9001 \\\n21\t  -e ARMOR_B2_REGION=us-east-005 \\\n22\t  -e ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=your-key-id \\\n23\t  -e ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=your-key-secret \\\n24\t  -e ARMOR_BUCKET=your-bucket \\\n25\t  -e ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN=your-cf-domain.example.com \\\n26\t  -e ARMOR_MEK=$(openssl rand -hex 32) \\\n27\t  -e ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY=my-access-key \\\n28\t  -e ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY=my-secret-key \\\n29\t  ronaldraygun/armor:0.1.43\n30\t```\n31\t\n32\t> **Note:** The ARMOR CI pipeline auto-bumps the VERSION file on every build and publishes the container image as `ronaldraygun/armor:<version>` to Docker Hub. Always pin to a specific version tag in production deployments. Use the latest published tag from [Docker Hub](https://hub.docker.com/r/ronaldraygun/armor/tags).\n33\t\n34\t### Client Configuration\n35\t\n36\tPoint any S3-compatible tool at ARMOR's listen address:\n37\t\n38\t```bash\n39\t# AWS CLI\n40\taws --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3 cp file.txt s3://bucket/key\n41\t\n42\t# boto3 (Python)\n43\timport boto3\n44\ts3 = boto3.client('s3',\n45\t    endpoint_url='http://localhost:9000',\n46\t    aws_access_key_id='my-access-key',\n47\t    aws_secret_access_key='my-secret-key')\n48\ts3.upload_file('local.txt', 'bucket', 'key')\n49\t\n50\t# DuckDB\n51\tINSTALL httpfs;\n52\tLOAD httpfs;\n53\tSET s3_endpoint='localhost:9000';\n54\tSET s3_access_key_id='my-access-key';\n55\tSET s3_secret_access_key='my-secret-key';\n56\tSELECT * FROM read_parquet('s3://bucket/data.parquet');\n57\t```\n58\t\n59\t## Cost Model\n60\t\n61\t| Component | Cost |\n62\t|-----------|------|\n63\t| Storage | ~$6–7/TB/month |\n64\t| Egress (via Cloudflare Bandwidth Alliance) | $0 |\n65\t| B2 API calls | $0 |\n66\t| Cloudflare (free plan) | $0 |\n67\t| **Total** | **~$6–7/TB/month** |\n68\t\n69\t## Architecture\n70\t\n71\t### Upload Path (direct to B2 — ingress is free)\n72\t\n73\t```\n74\t┌──────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n75\t│  Client   │────▶│    ARMOR     │────▶│    B2    │\n76\t│           │     │  encrypt +   │     │          │\n77\t│           │     │  upload      │     │          │\n78\t└──────────┘     └──────────────┘     └──────────┘\n79\t```\n80\t\n81\t### Download Path (through Cloudflare — egress is free)\n82\t\n83\t```\n84\t┌──────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌────────────┐     ┌──────────┐\n85\t│  Client   │◀───│ Cloudflare │◀───│  Cloudflare │◀───│    B2    │\n86\t│  ARMOR    │    │   Edge     │    │  PNI Link   │    │          │\n87\t│  decrypt  │    │  (cache)   │    │  (free)     │    │          │\n88\t└──────────┘     └────────────┘     └────────────┘     └──────────┘\n89\t```\n90\t\n91\t### DuckDB Query Path (seekable decryption)\n92\t\n93\tDuckDB issues byte-range GET requests for specific row groups and columns. ARMOR decrypts only the requested 64KB blocks, so column pruning and predicate pushdown remain effective:\n94\t\n95\t```\n96\tDuckDB                          ARMOR                       Cloudflare → B2\n97\t  │                                │                              │\n98\t  ├─ read footer (last 8 bytes) ──▶├─ Range GET (encrypted) ────▶│\n99\t  │◀── decrypted footer ──────────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n100\t  │                                │                              │\n101\t  ├─ read col_a, row group 3 ────▶├─ Range GET (3 blocks) ─────▶│\n102\t  │◀── decrypted column chunk ────┤◀── ciphertext ──────────────┤\n103\t  │                                │                              │\n104\t  └─ result set                    └                              └\n105\t```\n106\t\n107\t## Encryption Design\n108\t\n109\t```\n110\tMaster Key (MEK)\n111\t │  stored locally, never uploaded\n112\t │\n113\t └─▶ wraps ──▶ Data Encryption Key (DEK)\n114\t                │  random per-file, wrapped copy in B2 metadata\n115\t                │\n116\t                └─▶ encrypts ──▶ File Data\n117\t                                   AES-256-CTR, 64KB blocks\n118\t                                   per-block HMAC-SHA256\n119\t                                   seekable random access\n120\t```\n121\t\n122\tKey rotation re-wraps DEKs without re-uploading file data — a metadata-only operation.\n123\t\n124\t## Security Model\n125\t\n126\t| Threat | Mitigation |\n127\t|--------|-----------|\n128\t| B2 breach | Client-side encryption — B2 only stores opaque blobs |\n129\t| Cloudflare inspection | Client-side encryption — Cloudflare only caches opaque blobs |\n130\t| Man-in-the-middle | TLS everywhere + client-side encryption |\n131\t| Key compromise | Envelope encryption — per-file DEKs limit blast radius; rotation re-wraps without re-uploading |\n132\t| Data corruption | SHA-256 integrity hash + per-block HMACs |\n133\t| Unauthorized access | Private bucket + Cloudflare Worker auth + scoped application keys |\n134\t\n135\t## Configuration\n136\t\n137\tARMOR is configured via environment variables:\n138\t\n139\t| Variable | Required | Default | Description |\n140\t|----------|----------|---------|-------------|\n141\t| `ARMOR_LISTEN` | No | `0.0.0.0:9000` | S3 API listen address |\n142\t| `ARMOR_ADMIN_LISTEN` | No | `127.0.0.1:9001` | Admin API (key rotation, canary, audit) |\n143\t| `ARMOR_B2_REGION` | Yes | — | B2 region (e.g., `us-east-005`) |\n144\t| `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID` | Yes | — | B2 application key ID |\n145\t| `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY` | Yes | — | B2 application key |\n146\t| `ARMOR_BUCKET` | Yes | — | B2 bucket name |\n147\t| `ARMOR_CF_DOMAIN` | Yes | — | Cloudflare domain CNAME'd to B2 |\n148\t| `ARMOR_MEK` | Yes | — | Master encryption key (hex, 32 bytes) |\n149\t| `ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY` | No | (random) | Client access key |\n150\t| `ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY` | No | (random) | Client secret key |\n151\t| `ARMOR_BLOCK_SIZE` | No | `65536` | Encryption block size (bytes) |\n152\t| `ARMOR_WRITER_ID` | No | (hostname) | Provenance chain writer ID |\n153\t| `ARMOR_READYZ_CACHE_TTL` | No | `30` | Seconds to cache backend connectivity in `/readyz` |\n154\t| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_USER` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth username |\n155\t| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_PASS` | No | — | Dashboard HTTP Basic Auth password |\n156\t| `ARMOR_DASHBOARD_TOKEN` | No | — | Dashboard Bearer token |\n157\t\n158\t### Multi-Key Routing\n159\t\n160\tRoute different path prefixes to different master keys:\n161\t\n162\t```bash\n163\tARMOR_MEK=<hex>                           # default key\n164\tARMOR_MEK_SENSITIVE=<hex>                 # named key\n165\tARMOR_MEK_ARCHIVE=<hex>                   # named key\n166\tARMOR_KEY_ROUTES=\"data/pii/*=sensitive,archive/*=archive,*=default\"\n167\t```\n168\t\n169\t### Multi-Credential Configuration\n170\t\n171\t```bash\n172\tARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACCESS_KEY=reader-key\n173\tARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_SECRET_KEY=reader-secret\n174\tARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACL=\"mybucket:readonly/*\"\n175\t\n176\tARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACCESS_KEY=writer-key\n177\tARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_SECRET_KEY=writer-secret\n178\tARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACL=\"mybucket:*,otherbucket:uploads/*\"\n179\t```\n180\t\n181\t## S3 API Coverage\n182\t\n183\t### Transforming Operations (encryption/decryption applied)\n184\t\n185\t| Operation | Support |\n186\t|-----------|---------|\n187\t| PutObject | Full (streaming for large files) |\n188\t| GetObject | Full (range reads) |\n189\t| HeadObject | Full (plaintext size, conditionals) |\n190\t| CopyObject | Full (DEK re-wrapping, cross-bucket) |\n191\t| CreateMultipartUpload | Full |\n192\t| UploadPart | Full |\n193\t| CompleteMultipartUpload | Full |\n194\t| AbortMultipartUpload | Full |\n195\t| ListParts | Full |\n196\t| ListMultipartUploads | Full |\n197\t\n198\t### Passthrough Operations\n199\t\n200\t| Operation | Support |\n201\t|-----------|---------|\n202\t| ListObjectsV2 | Full (size correction, `.armor/` filter) |\n203\t| DeleteObject | Full |\n204\t| DeleteObjects | Full |\n205\t| ListBuckets | Full |\n206\t| CreateBucket / DeleteBucket / HeadBucket | Full |\n207\t| Lifecycle configuration | Full |\n208\t| Object Lock / Retention / Legal Hold | Full |\n209\t\n210\t**Reserved Namespace: `.armor/`**\n211\t\n212\tThe `.armor/` prefix is reserved for ARMOR internal use. Client operations targeting keys with this prefix return `403 AccessDenied`. This protects:\n213\t\n214\t- `.armor/chain/<writer>/*` — Tamper-evident provenance chain entries\n215\t- `.armor/chain-head/<writer>` — Provenance chain head pointers\n216\t- `.armor/manifest/<writer>/*` — Manifest delta files (IV + wrapped DEK entries)\n217\t- `.armor/hmac/<sha256>` — Multipart upload HMAC sidecars\n218\t- `.armor/rotation-state.json` — In-progress key rotation state\n219\t- `.armor/multipart/*.state` — Crash recovery state for multipart uploads\n220\t- `.armor/canary/*` — Health check canary objects\n221\t\n222\tInternal ARMOR components (provenance recorder, manifest persistence, canary, key rotation, multipart state manager) access these keys directly through the backend layer, bypassing the S3 handler guard.\n223\t\n224\t## Web Dashboard\n225\t\n226\tA web dashboard for bucket browsing, encryption status, and metrics is available on the admin port (default `127.0.0.1:9001`):\n227\t\n228\t```bash\n229\topen http://localhost:9001/dashboard\n230\t```\n231\t\n232\tFeatures:\n233\t- Bucket browsing with prefix-based navigation\n234\t- Encryption status badges per object (key name, ARMOR vs. unencrypted)\n235\t- Metadata cache hit rates\n236\t- Real-time metrics: requests, bytes transferred, uptime, canary status\n237\t\n238\tSee [docs/dashboard.md](docs/dashboard.md) for full documentation.\n239\t\n240\t## Admin API\n241\t\n242\tKey management and monitoring endpoints on the admin listener (`127.0.0.1:9001`):\n243\t\n244\t| Endpoint | Method | Description |\n245\t|----------|--------|-------------|\n246\t| `/healthz` | GET | Liveness check |\n247\t| `/readyz` | GET | Readiness check (verifies B2 connectivity) |\n248\t| `/metrics` | GET | Prometheus metrics |\n249\t| `/admin/key/verify` | GET | Verify MEK can decrypt the canary object |\n250\t| `/admin/key/rotate` | POST | Rotate MEK — re-wraps all DEKs, no file re-upload |\n251\t| `/admin/key/export` | GET | Export current MEK (`?confirm=yes`) |\n252\t| `/admin/audit` | GET | Walk provenance chains, verify integrity |\n253\t| `/admin/presign` | POST | Generate pre-signed share URL |\n254\t| `/armor/canary` | GET | Canary integrity status |\n255\t| `/dashboard` | GET | Web dashboard |\n256\t\n257\t## Repository Structure\n258\t\n259\t```\n260\tARMOR/\n261\t├── cmd/armor/main.go          # Entrypoint\n262\t├── internal/\n263\t│   ├── server/                # S3 API handlers, auth\n264\t│   ├── crypto/                # Encryption, decryption, envelope key management\n265\t│   ├── backend/               # B2 S3 client, Cloudflare download routing\n266\t│   ├── canary/                # Self-healing integrity monitor\n267\t│   ├── config/                # Configuration loading (env vars)\n268\t│   ├── keymanager/            # Multi-key routing\n269\t│   ├── dashboard/             # Web dashboard UI and metrics\n270\t│   ├── presign/               # Pre-signed URL generation\n271\t│   ├── provenance/            # Cryptographic audit chain\n272\t│   ├── logging/               # Structured JSON logging\n273\t│   └── metrics/               # Prometheus metrics\n274\t├── deploy/kubernetes/         # Kubernetes manifests\n275\t├── tests/integration/         # Integration tests (requires real B2 + Cloudflare)\n276\t└── docs/\n277\t    ├── dashboard.md\n278\t    ├── cloudflare-setup.md    # DNS configuration for zero-egress downloads\n279\t    └── research/\n280\t```\n281\t\n282\t## Documentation\n283\t\n284\t- [Disaster Recovery](docs/disaster-recovery.md) — MEK backup/escrow, restore drills, rotation failure recovery\n285\t- [Web Dashboard](docs/dashboard.md) — Bucket browsing, encryption status, cache statistics\n286\t- [Cloudflare Setup](docs/cloudflare-setup.md) — DNS configuration for zero-egress B2 downloads\n287\t- [Integration Tests](tests/integration/README.md) — Testing against real B2 + Cloudflare\n288\t\n289\t## Disaster Recovery / Offline Decryption\n290\t\n291\tARMOR includes a standalone CLI tool `armor-decrypt` for recovering encrypted objects without a running ARMOR server. This enables disaster recovery scenarios where you have:\n292\t\n293\t- The Master Encryption Key (MEK)\n294\t- Access to B2 (or a local copy of an encrypted object)\n295\t\n296\t### Building the Decrypt Tool\n297\t\n298\t```bash\n299\tgo build -o armor-decrypt ./cmd/armor-decrypt\n300\t```\n301\t\n302\t### Usage\n303\t\n304\t#### Decrypt from B2\n305\t\n306\t```bash\n307\t# Decrypt directly from B2 (requires B2 credentials)\n308\tarmor-decrypt \\\n309\t  -mek 0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef0123456789abcdef \\\n310\t  -input b2://my-bucket/path/to/file.encrypted \\\n311\t  -output recovered-file.txt\n312\t\n313\t# Using MEK from environment\n314\texport ARMOR_MEK=0123456789abcdef...\n315\tarmor-decrypt -input b2://my-bucket/file -output recovered.txt\n316\t\n317\t# With verbose output\n318\tarmor-decrypt -mek HEX -input b2://bucket/file -v - output recovered.txt\n319\t```\n320\t\n321\t#### Decrypt from Local File\n322\t\n323\tFor local files, you need the wrapped DEK (from `x-amz-meta-armor-wrapped-dek` metadata):\n324\t\n325\t```bash\n326\tarmor-decrypt \\\n327\t  -mek 0123456789abcdef... \\\n328\t  -input /path/to/encrypted.bin \\\n329\t  -wrapped-dek WWF...base64... \\\n330\t  -output plaintext.bin\n331\t```\n332\t\n333\t### Key Requirements\n334\t\n335\t- **MEK (Master Encryption Key)**: 32-byte hex string\n336\t- **For B2**: `ARMOR_B2_REGION`, `ARMOR_B2_ENDPOINT`, `ARMOR_B2_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, `ARMOR_B2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY`\n337\t- **For local files**: Wrapped DEK (base64, from object metadata)\n338\t\n339\t### Multi-Key Support\n340\t\n341\tIf your ARMOR deployment uses named keys (via `ARMOR_KEY_ROUTES`), specify the key ID:\n342\t\n343\t```bash\n344\tarmor-decrypt \\\n345\t  -mek <hex-for-specific-key> \\\n346\t  -input b2://bucket/file \\\n347\t  -key-id sensitive \\\n348\t  -output recovered.txt\n349\t```\n350\t\n351\tThe key ID comes from the `x-amz-meta-armor-key-id` metadata header.\n352\t\n353\t### Verification\n354\t\n355\tThe decrypt tool automatically:\n356\t\n357\t- Verifies per-block HMAC-SHA256 integrity\n358\t- Validates the plaintext SHA-256 checksum\n359\t- Detects corrupted blocks or wrong MEK\n360\t\n361\tExit codes:\n362\t- `0`: Success\n363\t- `1`: Decryption failed (wrong MEK, corrupted data, HMAC mismatch)\n364\t\n365\t### Example Workflow\n366\t\n367\t```bash\n368\t# 1. List objects to find the target\n369\taws s3 ls --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 s3://bucket/\n370\t\n371\t# 2. Get metadata to see key requirements\n372\taws s3api head-object --endpoint-url http://localhost:9000 \\\n373\t  --bucket bucket --key file\n374\t\n375\t# 3. Decrypt with the correct MEK\n376\tarmor-decrypt -mek $ARMOR_MEK -input b2://bucket/file -output recovered\n377\t\n378\t# 4. Verify the recovered file\n379\tsha256sum recovered  # Should match x-amz-meta-armor-plaintext-sha256\n380\t```\n381\t\n382\t## License\n383\t\n384\tMIT\n385\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_55c2a797caf54544b808f918","session_id":"87d1ceba-3753-46c2-8019-12a1aa96818c","uuid":"31592a7a-5d1e-4d3a-9c63-b28d7df26d7a","timestamp":"2026-07-15T16:20:13.939Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore credential-related code"}

What \"Multi-Credential Configuration\" Means\n\nMulti-credential configuration in ARMOR allows you to define multiple client authentication credentials with different access levels and permissions. This enables:\n- Multiple clients/users to authenticate with different credentials\n- Fine-grained access control through ACLs (Access Control Lists)\n- Credential isolation and security segmentation\n- Support for credential rotation scenarios\n\n### 2. Main Credential Handling Code Locations\n\n**Core Configuration:**\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/config/config.go` (lines 25-30, 70-71, 169-190, 379-490)\n  - Defines the `Credential` struct with `AccessKey`, `SecretKey`, and `ACLs`\n  - Defines the `ACLEntry` struct for bucket/prefix permissions\n  - Handles loading credentials from environment variables\n  - Supports both default and named credentials\n\n**Authentication Logic:**\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/auth.go` (lines 18-47, 116-165, 293-318)\n  - `SigV4Auth` struct with multi-credential support\n  - `NewSigV4AuthWithCredentials()` function for multiple credentials\n  - `VerifyRequest()` function for signature validation\n  - `CheckACL()` function for permission validation\n\n**Server Integration:**\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/server.go` (lines 42, 67, 70)\n  - Server initialization uses `s.config.Credentials` map\n  - `verifyAuthAndGetCredential()` function creates auth with all credentials\n\n### 3. How Multi-Credential Mode Works\n\n**Credential Definition (Environment Variables):**\n```bash\n# Default credential (auto-generated if not provided)\nARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY=<default-key>\nARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY=<default-secret>\n\n# Named credentials with specific permissions\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACCESS_KEY=reader-key\nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_SECRET_KEY=reader-secret  \nARMOR_AUTH_READONLY_ACL=\"mybucket:readonly/*\"\n\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACCESS_KEY=writer-key\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_SECRET_KEY=writer-secret\nARMOR_AUTH_WRITER_ACL=\"mybucket:*,otherbucket:uploads/*\"\n```\n\n**ACL Format:**\n- Format: `bucket:prefix`\n- Wildcard bucket `\"*\"` means all buckets\n- Empty prefix `\"\"` or `\"*\"` means any key within the bucket\n- Multiple ACLs separated by commas: `\"bucket1:prefix1,bucket2:prefix2\"`\n\n**Configuration Loading:**\n1. Default credential loaded from `ARMOR_AUTH_ACCESS_KEY` and `ARMOR_AUTH_SECRET_KEY`\n2. Additional credentials scanned from `ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACCESS_KEY` pattern\n3. Each credential gets its ACLs parsed from `ARMOR_AUTH_<NAME>_ACL`\n4. All credentials stored in `cfg.Credentials` map (access key -> credential)\n\n**Authentication Flow:**\n1. Client request includes AWS SigV4 authentication header\n2. Server extracts access key from authorization header\n3. Looks up credential in credentials map\n4. Verifies signature using the credential's secret key\n5. Checks ACLs for bucket/prefix access permissions\n6. Grants or denies access based on validation results\n\n### 4. Existing Test Coverage for Credentials\n\n**Test Files:**\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/auth_test.go` - Unit tests for auth logic\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/multi_credential_integration_test.go` - Multi-credential integration tests\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/auth_integration_test.go` - Auth integration tests\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/server/invalid_credential_test.go` - Invalid credential tests\n\n**Test Coverage:**\n- Multiple credential sets authentication\n- Credential isolation between different users\n- ACL validation for bucket/prefix restrictions\n- Credential rotation scenarios\n- Concurrent requests with different credentials\n- Edge cases (empty credentials, large numbers, complex ACLs)\n- Signature verification and validation\n- Wildcard bucket and prefix ACLs\n\n### 5. Configuration Structure\n\n**Data Structures:**\n```go\ntype Credential struct {\n    AccessKey string\n    SecretKey string  \n    ACLs      []ACLEntry\n}\n\ntype ACLEntry struct {\n    Bucket string // \"*\" for all buckets\n    Prefix string // \"\" or \"*\" for any prefix\n}\n\ntype Config struct {\n    // Single credential support\n    AuthAccessKey string\n    AuthSecretKey string\n    \n    // Multi-credential support\n    Credentials map[string]*Credential // Access key -> Credential\n    \n    // ... other configuration fields\n}\n```\n\n**Key Features:**\n- Credentials stored as map for O(1) lookup by access key\n- ACLs are optional (nil means full access)\n- Support for both single and multi-credential modes\n- Environment-based configuration with validation\n- Automatic duplicate detection\n\n### 6. Configuration Files for Multi-Credential Mode\n\n**Kubernetes Deployment:**\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/deploy/kubernetes/deployment.yaml` (lines 71-80)\n  - Shows default credential setup from Kubernetes secrets\n  - Can be extended for multiple credentials\n\n**Docker/Environment:**\n- `/home/coding/ARMOR/README.md` (lines 171-179)\n  - Documents multi-credential configuration examples\n  - Shows ACL format and usage\n\nThe ARMOR multi-credential system provides a robust, production-ready authentication and authorization mechanism with fine-grained access control, comprehensive test coverage, and flexible configuration options."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":0},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_55c2a797caf54544b808f918","session_id":"87d1ceba-3753-46c2-8019-12a1aa96818c","uuid":"29b88420-343e-43bb-bb9f-c752917cd216","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Explore credential-related code"}
